Tests the behavior of the `arguments` object in a function by checking an out-of-bounds index.

# Purpose
This code defines a function `f_arg` that returns the `arguments` object, which is an array-like object containing the arguments passed to the function. The code then uses an `assert` statement to verify that accessing the fourth element (index 3) of the `arguments` object returned by `f_arg` results in `undefined`, as only three arguments are passed to the function. This script provides narrow functionality, focusing on demonstrating the behavior of the `arguments` object in JavaScript.
